## Description

<!--
What does this PR do? Why is this change needed?
Keep it concise — a paragraph is usually enough.
-->

## Related Issue

<!--
Every PR should close or reference at least one issue.
Use one of:
  Closes #<issue-number>
  Refs #<issue-number>
-->

Closes #

## Type of Change

<!--
Delete options that are not relevant.
-->

- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix
- [ ] ✨ New feature
- [ ] 🔧 Chore / infrastructure
- [ ] 📖 Documentation
- [ ] ♻️ Refactor (no behaviour change)
- [ ] ⚡ Performance improvement
- [ ] 🧪 Tests only

## Testing Done

<!--
Describe how you tested your changes.
Include commands run, test scenarios covered, or manual steps taken.
-->

- [ ] Unit tests pass locally (`pytest` / `npm test`)
- [ ] Manually tested the affected flow end-to-end
- [ ] No regressions observed in adjacent functionality

## Screenshots / Recordings

<!--
For UI changes, include before/after screenshots or a short screen recording.
Delete this section if not applicable.
-->

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| <!-- screenshot --> | <!-- screenshot --> |

## Checklist

- [ ] My branch follows the naming convention (`feat/`, `fix/`, `chore/`, `docs/`, …)
- [ ] My commits follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/)
- [ ] I have linked this PR to a related issue
- [ ] I have updated documentation where relevant (README, docstrings, etc.)
- [ ] I have not introduced unrelated changes (one logical change per PR)
- [ ] I am happy for this to be squash-merged under the PR title

## Additional Notes

<!--
Anything the reviewer should know before reviewing, e.g. follow-up work planned, known limitations, or decisions made.
-->
